lyrics:
You held their words like sacred lines
Swore their wisdom would stand through time
Every promise, every vow
Now their echoes are fading somehow

But the doubts grew, the cracks ran deep
A silence where authority used to speak

They traded the truth for power and gain
Silenced the voices that called out their name
And when you let go of the grace you had found
The heavens surrendered and the gods fell down

They taught you well, they wrote the rules
Said knowledge saves and faith renews
They led the march, they raised the sound
But where are they now it's come down

But the doubts grew, the cracks ran deep
A silence where authority used to speak

They traded the truth for their own good
Ignored the prophets, replaced where they stood
And when you gave up all the grace you had found
The heavens surrendered and the gods fell down

What do you hold when the fire is cold
What do you trust when the lies unfold
Where do you turn when the echoes fade
Or do you stand in the ruins you made

They traded your faith for their own good
Ignored the prophets, replaced where they stood
And when you abandoned the grace you had found
The heavens surrendered and the gods fell down

The heavens surrendered... and the gods fell down
persona:
## 4Castle

The Future of Rock, The Power of Harmony

**4Castle **is a genre-defying, five-piece supergroup under Maricopa Records, built on a foundation of powerful vocal harmonies, intricate musicianship, and fearless innovation. Rooted in rock, but unbound by limitations, their music fuses elements of progressive rock, funk, Latin rhythms, blues, and folk, delivering a sound that is both technically complex and emotionally resonant.

### **A Band Like No Other**

Each member of **4Castle **brings a distinct voice-both musically and vocally-to the mix:
🎸 **Raven Cortez**(Lead Guitar, Vocals) – A fiery virtuoso with Latin and blues influences, weaving face-melting solos into every performance.
🎸 **Skylar “Sky” Monroe**(Rhythm Guitar, Vocals) – The poetic soul of the band, crafting melodic foundations and lyrically rich storytelling.
🎹 **Jonah Vega**(Keyboards, Vocals) – The sonic architect, layering psychedelic synths, Hammond organ swells, and cinematic orchestrations.
🎸 **Malcolm “Mack” Bishop**(Bass, Vocals) – The groove master, blending funk, jazz, and progressive rock into fluid, deep-pocket basslines.
🥁 **Eli “Fox” Foster **(Drums, Vocals) – The wild heartbeat, pushing the band forward with thunderous fills, polyrhythmic grooves, and explosive energy.

### **What Defines 4Castle?**

- **Multi-Layered Vocal Harmonies**– All five members **sing**, creating **rich, choral-like textures**that elevate every song.

- **Complex Arrangements & Bold Songwriting**– Each track is an **immersive journey**, balancing **technical mastery with raw emotion**.

- **Rhythmic Power & Innovation**– From **tight funk grooves to shifting time signatures**, their music **moves you-literally and figuratively**.

- **Lyrically Challenging Yet Accessible**– Their songs explore **deep themes of life, change, and resilience**, wrapped in **memorable hooks and melodies**.

### **4Castle’s Sound**

Imagine putting Emerson, Lake & Palmer, Heart, Led Zeppelin, Sly Stone, and Carlos Santana in a blender. The result? **4Castle**. Their music pays homage to the greats while pushing rock’s boundaries into the future.

### **Join the Journey**

With Maricopa Records, **4Castle **is set to redefine what a modern rock band can be. Expect genre-bending albums, electrifying performances, and music that resonates across generations.

🚀 Follow **4Castle **as they build their legacy-one song, one harmony, and one unforgettable moment at a time.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Cmxf7xuTHzf7UahMK2xJh)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/4castle/1801052673)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/5-rooks-in-a-dark-setting-1.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This modern rock track creates an atmosphere of earnest disillusionment, opening with a textured, bluesy electric guitar melody before the lead vocal enters. The energy level is highly dynamic, building from a controlled, pensive verse into a massive, anthemic chorus with a driving, full-band feel. The production is polished and spacious, allowing the wide, layered guitars, powerful drums, and prominent bass to coexist without sounding cluttered, all while keeping the lead vocal clearly at the forefront. The defining quality of the song is its explosive dynamic shift into the chorus, where the emotional weight of the lyrics is matched by the sheer force of the arrangement, especially at the powerful lyrical climax, "and the gods fell down."

review.review_text: Opening on a lean, bluesy guitar figure before the full band arrives like a verdict, 'And the Gods Fell Down' builds its case with patience and then lands it hard: that chorus drop in D major, at a brisk 152 BPM, hits with the kind of weight that earns the title. The lyric 'what do you hold when the fire is cold' is the kind of plain-spoken question that cuts deeper than clever wordplay, and the way the arrangement opens up beneath it, guitars wide, bass prominent, the dynamic shift feeling earned rather than engineered, recalls the Foo Fighters at their most purposeful. A highlight.
anchors_used: lyric: 'what do you hold when the fire is cold'
